query_normalized: statistical analysis of publicly available weather data for extreme event prediction statistics
rationale: The paper focuses on statistical methodology for handling censored data in extreme value theory (specifically applied to brain cancer data), but it does not address the user's specific domain of "publicly available weather data" or the application to "extreme event prediction" in meteorology. While it shares the general topic of extreme value statistics, it fails the inclusion criteria because it does not measure the same mechanism on the user's domain (weather), nor does it provide a canonical
